canto_title: The Vision of the Three Lights and the Revelation of Hope

scenes.summary: Dante expresses his desire for his poem to overcome cruelty and returns to the site of his spiritual baptism.

scenes.scene_name: The Appearance of the Second Light
scenes.summary: A second light descends toward the poets, and Beatrice points out the figure of Hope.
Dante compares the affectionate greeting between the two great figures to two doves communicating.
Beatrice laughs and calls for the resonance of Hope to reach the heights of heaven.
Beatrice instructs Dante to lift his head and prepare himself for the truth of the mortal world.
The second light commands Dante to identify the nature and origin of Hope.

scenes.scene_name: The Nature of Hope in the Church
scenes.summary: Beatrice explains that Hope is granted to the Church Militant as a way to glimpse Jerusalem.
Dante explains that Hope is a certain expectation of glory and traces its source to the light of St. John.
A sudden flash of lightning occurs, and the third light asks Dante to speak of what Hope promises.
Dante uses the prophecies of Isaiah and the Revelation to identify the white robes of the saints.
The celestial choir responds in unison to Dante's words.

scenes.scene_name: The Arrival of the Third Light
scenes.summary: A third, blindingly bright light appears, moving like a joyful maiden into the center of the circle.
Beatrice identifies the third light as the figure who died on the cross for humanity's sake.
Beatrice falls into a state of silent, concentrated focus following her words.

scenes.scene_name: The Prophecy of the Two Lights
scenes.summary: The third light speaks of its earthly body and predicts the importance of the two lights that will ascend.
The celestial rotation quiets and settles, much like oars being stilled after a signal.
Dante experiences intense emotional agitation as he tries to look at Beatrice and fails.
